summary: VEP cache version mismatched with PyEnsembl release (critical). Search database 4.7x reference proteome size without class-specific FDR. FASTA headers lack variant position metadata required for protein grouping.

findings.title: VEP cache version mismatched with PyEnsembl release
findings.message: VEP configured with Ensembl release 108 cache (--cache_version 108) but PyEnsembl genome object constructed with release=106 (EnsemblRelease(106)). VEP annotates variant consequences using release 108 transcript models; PyEnsembl translates those transcript IDs using release 106 sequences. For transcripts restructured between releases 106 and 108, protein sequences will be generated from the wrong CDS.
findings.impact: Protein sequences for variants on reannotated transcripts will be incorrect. The error is silent — protein generation succeeds but produces sequences that do not correspond to any biological entity. Mass spectrometry will never detect these phantom peptides, deflating sensitivity without any error signal.
findings.recommendation: Pin VEP cache and PyEnsembl to the same Ensembl release. Add a validation step asserting vep_cache_version == pyensembl_release at pipeline startup. Recommended: Ensembl 110 (GRCh38) for both.
findings.code_snippet:
# vep_annotation.py line 15
vep_cmd = ['vep', '--cache_version', '108', '--assembly', 'GRCh38', ...]

# protein_generation.py line 89
genome = EnsemblRelease(106)  # MISMATCH: VEP uses 108

findings.title: Search database 4.7x reference proteome — FDR impact not quantified
findings.message: build_fasta.py generates 112,400 protein sequences from VCF variants. The canonical UniProt reference proteome (human, reviewed) contains 20,371 entries. Ratio: 4.7x. No class-specific FDR is applied in the downstream search configuration — standard 1% PSM-level FDR is applied globally across both reference and variant peptide classes.
findings.impact: Actual FDR for variant peptide class is approximately 4.7x nominal (Nesvizhskii 2014). At nominal 1% FDR, actual variant-class FDR is ~4.7%. For a study finding 500 variant peptides, ~23 are expected to be false positives under correct accounting.
findings.recommendation: Apply class-specific FDR (separate target-decoy for reference vs variant classes) or reduce DB size by filtering variants to high-confidence coding changes with AF > 0.001. See proteomics-reviewer findings for search configuration.
references: Nesvizhskii AI (2014) Proteogenomics: concepts, applications and computational strategies. Nat Methods 11(11):1114-1125

findings.title: FASTA headers lack variant position and traceability metadata
findings.message: Generated FASTA headers follow format '>VAR_{transcript_id}_{aa_change}' (e.g., '>VAR_ENST00000366667_p.Val600Glu'). Headers do not include: genomic coordinates (chr:pos:ref:alt), original VCF ID field, zygosity, or population frequency. Downstream protein grouping in MaxQuant/Spectronaut cannot trace identified peptides back to specific genomic variants.
findings.impact: Protein grouping at the variant level is impossible. Multiple variant proteins with different genomic origins but identical amino acid changes will be merged. Variant peptide candidates detected by MS cannot be mapped back to specific VCF records for biological interpretation.
findings.recommendation: Extend FASTA headers: '>VAR|{vcf_id}|{chr}:{pos}:{ref}:{alt}|{transcript_id}|{aa_change}|AF={af}'. Verify the extended header format is compatible with the search engine's FASTA parser (check proteomics-reviewer for search header validation).

findings.title: Chromosome prefix mismatch: VCF uses 'chr' prefix but VEP cache expects bare numbers
findings.message: Input VCF files use UCSC-style chromosome naming ('chr1', 'chr2', ..., 'chrX'). VEP cache is Ensembl-style (1, 2, ..., X). No prefix stripping is performed before VEP annotation. VEP will silently drop all contigs with unrecognised names, producing an output VCF with 0 annotated variants — no error is raised.
findings.impact: If not caught before downstream steps, protein generation pipeline receives an empty annotation file and produces zero variant proteins. If silently handled by default values, all variants are annotated as 'UNKNOWN' consequence. Either outcome invalidates the custom database.
findings.recommendation: Add chromosome prefix normalisation before VEP: use bcftools annotate --rename-chrs to convert UCSC to Ensembl naming. Or use VEP --chr flag to specify expected chromosome format. Add an assertion: output VCF variant count must be >= 90% of input variant count.
findings.code_snippet:
# VCF header shows: ##contig=<ID=chr1,length=248956422>
# VEP GRCh38 cache expects: 1, 2, ..., X, Y, MT
vep_cmd = ['vep', '--input_file', vcf, ...]  # no chr prefix handling

findings.title: NMD-susceptible truncated proteins included in search database
findings.message: Frameshift and stop-gain variants produce truncated protein sequences that are included in the database without NMD filtering. Transcripts satisfying the '50-55 nt rule' (premature termination codon >50-55 nt upstream of last exon-exon junction) are predicted NMD substrates. Corresponding proteins are not expressed in vivo.
findings.impact: MBR can transfer identifications from NMD-susceptible phantom proteins across runs, creating false consistency. Quantification of these proteins produces artefactual differential expression results.
findings.recommendation: Apply NMD prediction (e.g., NMDterminus, NMDetective) and exclude NMD-predicted transcripts from protein generation. At minimum, flag NMD-predicted proteins in FASTA headers for post-search filtering.

findings.title: No class-specific FDR configured for reference vs variant peptide classes
findings.message: search_config.yaml sets fdr_threshold: 0.01 as a single global threshold applied across all PSMs. With a 4.7x inflated database, the decoy competition is dominated by reference sequences. Variant peptides compete for a disproportionately small fraction of the decoy budget, resulting in underestimated FDR for the variant class.
findings.impact: Estimated 4.7x FDR inflation for variant class (see PG-2). Novel variant peptide identifications carry approximately 4.7% false discovery rate at nominal 1% threshold.
findings.recommendation: Implement separated target-decoy analysis: run search with reference-only DB, identify confident reference PSMs, then run second pass with variant sequences against remaining spectra. Or use PeptideShaker's protein FDR with database partitioning.
findings.code_snippet:
# config/search_config.yaml
fdr:
  psm_threshold: 0.01
  peptide_threshold: 0.01
  protein_threshold: 0.01
  # MISSING: class_specific_fdr: true

findings.title: VEP --pick discards alternative transcript consequences
findings.message: VEP configured with --pick which selects a single 'best' transcript per variant and discards all others. For proteogenomics purposes, isoform-level analysis requires all transcript consequences to be considered for protein generation. Variants with severe consequences only on non-picked transcripts will be silently excluded from protein generation.
findings.impact: Isoform-specific variant proteins missed. Variants causing splice disruption or frameshift only in alternative isoforms will generate only reference protein. Coverage of the variant protein space is reduced but no error is raised.
findings.recommendation: Use --per_gene instead of --pick, or remove transcript selection entirely, and apply consequence filtering downstream. Alternatively, use --flag_pick to retain all transcripts while flagging the selected one for reporting.

findings.title: VEP cache version not explicitly pinned in pipeline config
findings.message: VEP cache directory is set as /data/vep_cache/ without specifying the release version in the path or config. If the cache directory is updated in-place (e.g., during Ensembl release cycle), the pipeline will silently use a different release without any config change.
findings.impact: Non-reproducible results between pipeline runs separated by a cache update. Previous result sets cannot be exactly replicated.
findings.recommendation: Use release-versioned cache paths: /data/vep_cache/108/ and pin the version in config as a documented parameter. Include VEP cache release in pipeline provenance output.
